summary: Enumerate quant/HFT firms by region to complete the FIRMS.md universe

new_firms.compute_signal: Job ads for 'AI Lab researcher' / 'AI algorithm engineer' on financial time-series prediction, alpha-factor mining and portfolio optimization; ML-driven since 2019. No standalone DC disclosed.
new_firms.scale: ~RMB 60-70bn AUM band; #1 百亿量化 performer 2025
new_firms.note: Founded 2011 Hangzhou by ex-BGI/BlackRock quants; one of China's oldest quant hedge funds. Shifted to mid/high-freq ML in 2019. Holds HK SFC asset-mgmt licence.

new_firms.compute_signal: Founder describes full-process AI from signal discovery to model construction, using LLMs to parse text data; recruits ML researchers with AI-lab/Kaggle backgrounds. Footprint not quantified.
new_firms.scale: RMB 60-70bn AUM band; top-5 by size
new_firms.note: Founded 2014 by Stanford grads Zou Yitian and Chen Zehao; deploying AI/ML since 2017. Consistently top-10 in the 2025 百亿量化 performance list.

new_firms.compute_signal: Publicly stated 2023 shift to an ML-centred non-linear modelling framework; ~80% of ~80 staff are R&D/IT across Beijing/Shenzhen/Shanghai. No DC disclosure.
new_firms.note: Founded 2013 by He Wenqi (ex-Millennium senior researcher); research director was WorldQuant China GM. Crossed RMB 10bn in 2020. Also styled 'Alphatecture'.

new_firms.compute_signal: Strongest public compute signal in tier: cumulative ~RMB 200m on GPUs/servers since 2016; relocated its 'Constellation' supercomputer base to Shanghai Pudong targeting ~80% annual compute growth; in-house AI Lab.
new_firms.scale: RMB 10bn+ (百亿量化); consistent top-10 excess-return
new_firms.note: High-frequency-origin quant manager, English name commonly 'Mengxi' (distinct from 鸣石 Mingshi Fund). Public GPU/supercomputer spend is directly on-thesis for a compute-footprint map.

new_firms.compute_signal: Recruits quant researchers (Python/C++, 3yr+); part of 2025 quant-AI hiring wave. No public DC/GPU quantification.
new_firms.note: Founded 2015 Shenzhen, USTC-alumni partners; proprietary-trading roots across both stock exchanges plus four futures exchanges.

new_firms.scale: RMB 10bn+ tier; peaked ~RMB 35bn on DMA then shrank
new_firms.note: Founded 2014 Shanghai; CTA/managed-futures heritage (top-3 private CTA by size in 2021), ~70-person research team. Size-volatile 百亿量化 member after 2024 small-cap drawdown.

new_firms.compute_signal: Runs 'Tidal Plan' training program citing an in-house full-stack system and 'massive data support'; recruits quant + AI talent. No DC disclosure.
new_firms.note: Founded end-2020 by Sun Lin and Yu Hang (10yr+ overseas quant backgrounds); index-enhancement, market-neutral and CTA lines. Fast riser into the 百亿量化 tier.

new_firms.compute_signal: Publicly cited (with Ubiquant) as among first Chinese quants to build out AI teams and compete for AI talent; ~116-person team. No DC/GPU figures public.
new_firms.note: Founded 2014 by Xu Shunan (Tsinghua/MIT; ex-IMC HK). ~70% of PMs home-grown. Registered as 因诺(上海) but Beijing-rooted; in the 百亿量化 tier.

new_firms.compute_signal: Self-describes as focused on 'AI quantitative investment'; stock model uses 10,000+ factors incl. alternative data (order-book, sentiment, forum/analyst text). No DC disclosure.
new_firms.note: Founded 2015 by physics-PhD Wang Hongyong with USTC classmates; grew 30bn->100bn in ~13 months. In the 百亿量化 roster.

new_firms.compute_signal: Describes math + AI as core tech; ultra-high-frequency futures origin. No public DC/GPU figures.
new_firms.scale: RMB 10bn tier (moves in and out of 百亿)
new_firms.note: Founded 2015 Shanghai; started in ultra-HF futures, entered equities 2018. Strategies span index-enhancement, neutral, T0, futures/options HF, CTA.

new_firms.compute_signal: Self-describes proprietary low-latency trading system for fully-automated algorithmic trading; index-enhancement focus. Latency-first; no public DC/GPU figures.
new_firms.note: Founded 2016 by Dr Zhang Zhuo; ~70+ staff, 80% research. Received a Shenzhen Exchange regulatory letter in 2024 over IPO-subscription violations. Beijing-HQ'd.

new_firms.compute_signal: Founder frames strategy around 'AI and continuous innovation'; model system blends linear and non-linear. No DC/GPU disclosure.
new_firms.note: Founded 2010; founder Yuan Yu (Wharton PhD). ~100 staff, 70% research; ~70% equity strategies. Distinct from 蒙玺 (also romanised Mingshi). AMAC-registered.
Controlled by Wang Yiping; one of few China managers spanning discretionary and quant. A hybrid rather than pure-ML shop, so classed Systematic.
Registered as 宁波金戈量锐, founded Nov-2014 by 'Jin Ge' (USTC/Columbia MFE; ex-Laurion Capital). Index-enhancement + market-neutral. AMAC-registered.

new_firms.scale: 70+ employees; offices Mumbai, Kolkata, Bangalore, GIFT City
new_firms.note: Founded 2009 shortly after DMA in Indian markets; one of India's longest-running HFT/market-making prop shops. Latency/colo-first; no public GPU/DC signal.

new_firms.scale: One of India's largest prop trading firms
new_firms.note: Founded 2007 by Sudeep Gupta. Quantitative prop trading across all asset classes on global exchanges. Latency-first; no public standalone compute/DC disclosure.

new_firms.compute_signal: HPC job ads — public HPC Engineer / ML Researcher postings; in-house high-performance clusters
new_firms.note: Founded 2014 by Ankit Gupta, IIT-trained. Prop quant/HFT across asset classes. Public HPC Engineer/ML Researcher postings indicate in-house high-performance compute — a genuine research-compute signal.

new_firms.scale: 250+ staff across ~10 offices; trades on 30+ exchanges
new_firms.note: Founded 2010 by Mohit Mutreja and Parshant Mittal (UPenn). Multi-asset quant prop trading with HFT origins; launched AlphaGrep Investment Management arm. No public DC/GPU disclosure.

new_firms.compute_signal: self-reported research compute — 'petabyte-scale data infrastructure and millions of compute cores' for research/backtesting
new_firms.note: Founded 2011 by Faraz Khan and Sudhanshu Narang. HFT prop firm; ultra-low-latency infra plus a stated large research-compute footprint for backtesting. Self-reported from own site.

new_firms.scale: SEBI-registered; flagship market-neutral product I-Alpha since 2009
new_firms.note: Founded 2008 by Sandeep Tyagi. Quant asset management (market-neutral for HNIs/family offices), prop trading, and execution/market-making. Classed Quant AM as the AM arm is the distinguishing feature.

new_firms.scale: Listed group entity Dolat Algotech Ltd; significant NSE derivatives volume
new_firms.note: Roots to 1971; modern entity is a tech-driven multi-strategy quant/HFT prop trader, NSE broker in cash and F&O, running low-latency algo trading. One of the earliest Indian algo pioneers.
Founded 2001; prop HFT firm, reportedly first to use NSE co-location. Trades all asset classes with sub-second holding periods. Latency/colo-first.

new_firms.compute_signal: none public (ML/technology emphasis stated, no DC/GPU disclosure)
new_firms.scale: Offices Delhi, Bengaluru, Mumbai, Gandhinagar, London, Hong Kong
new_firms.note: Founded by Ankit Singh and Ruchir Bindal; began trading Indian exchanges 2009, expanded globally. Strictly proprietary. Describes ML/technology confluence but no public standalone compute footprint.

new_firms.compute_signal: none public (systematic-alpha / AI-ML stated, no DC/GPU disclosure)
new_firms.scale: Founded 2020; rapidly grew to a top-5 India HFT by self-account
new_firms.note: Founded 2020 by Prashant Singh. Systematic alpha research and electronic market-making across asset classes; describes AI/ML-driven HFT market-making. In-house low-latency infra.

new_firms.scale: Lower public profile; LLP registered in India
new_firms.note: Founded 2020 by Aravind Nadarajan. Quantitative trading firm focused on market inefficiencies with technology. Newer, low-public-profile Indian quant shop.

new_firms.compute_signal: ML-based HFT platform per own site; hires for high-performance data infrastructure roles, but no public DC/GPU disclosure
new_firms.note: HQ (not satellite). Israel's flagship home-grown HFT prop firm, founded 2001 in a Herzliya garage. Self-describes an ML-based algorithmic HFT platform. Strongest genuinely-local Middle East quant HQ.

new_firms.compute_signal: execution-only (in-house latency-sensitive market-making system since 2010; microsecond execution claimed)
new_firms.note: HQ (not satellite). Israeli group that began as manual prop trading and built a proprietary low-latency market-making system in-house; official market maker on exchanges worldwide.

new_firms.compute_signal: none public (self-describes ML-driven medium-frequency strategies; no DC/GPU disclosure)
new_firms.note: HQ (not satellite). Small Tel Aviv ML-first, medium-frequency futures fund (CME equity indices, commodities, FX, Treasuries) founded 2020 by ex-Israeli prop alumni. Emerging.

new_firms.compute_signal: execution-only (algorithmic crypto market-making across CEX/DEX; no DC/GPU disclosure)
new_firms.note: HQ (Tel Aviv, founded 2018). Digital-asset algorithmic trading / market-making firm. Some corporate structuring in Gibraltar but team is Tel Aviv-based.

new_firms.compute_signal: execution-only (proprietary non-custodial execution tech 'Quant Pro'; crypto-derivative strategies)
new_firms.note: HQ (Ra'anana, Israel, since 2016). Quantitative digital-asset investment / market-making tech firm. Registered CTA with US CFTC/NFA.

new_firms.firm: Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA) — Quantitative R&D
new_firms.compute_signal: high-performance & quantum computing named in mandate; runs ADIA Lab (data science / ML / HPC research), but no public data-centre/GPU-fleet disclosure
new_firms.scale: SWF (~$1trn AUM class); QRD team 100+ people
new_firms.note: HQ / sovereign in-house desk. ADIA's Quantitative R&D group, headed by Marcos Lopez de Prado, builds systematic strategies internally and spun up ADIA Lab. Not a standalone fund; an internal department of the SWF.

new_firms.compute_signal: press notes investment in localized data centers / AI-driven execution engines; nothing primary/confirmed
new_firms.note: Newly domiciled in the UAE (ADGM/Dubai), so a local HQ rather than a satellite — but founder Nikolay Aleksandrov is ex-Millennium/ExodusPoint and anchor capital is Western. Emerging; treat as reported, not confirmed.

new_firms.compute_signal: execution-only (self-describes proprietary low-latency trading engine + 'best-in-class computing environment'; no DC/GPU disclosure)
new_firms.scale: VC-backed startup (Arbor Ventures, VentureSouq, Nuwa Capital, Access Bridge)
new_firms.note: HQ (Riyadh). Home-grown Saudi capital-markets / proprietary market-making firm providing liquidity to the Saudi Exchange (Tadawul). One of the few genuinely local Gulf quant/MM HQs; small and early-stage.

new_firms.local_name: ダルマ・キャピタル株式会社
new_firms.compute_signal: execution-only (JPX/PTS co-location, latency-first) plus a notable public R&D signal: 2021 joint experiment with Toshiba applying the Simulated Bifurcation Machine (pseudo-quantum optimization) to HFT on Japanese equities
new_firms.scale: Not publicly disclosed (small independent prop MM)
new_firms.note: The single domestic Japanese-HQ'd high-speed trader on the FSA 高速取引行為者 register — every other of ~52 registrants is a foreign firm's entity. Founded 2015 by ex-Lehman Tokyo trader Akiyoshi Shiotani.

new_firms.local_name: 株式会社GCIアセット・マネジメント
new_firms.compute_signal: Public ML/AI signal: firm turned a refurbished Kyoto temple into a big-data / AI research lab (Bloomberg, 2019); markets itself as translating academic research into systematic strategies. No named GPU/DC build public.
new_firms.note: Tokyo independent quantitative/systematic manager (est. 2000, founder Hideki Yamauchi), flagship GCI Systematic Macro Fund. One of the few genuinely systematic domestic Japanese HQs.

new_firms.local_name: シンプレクス・アセット・マネジメント株式会社
new_firms.scale: One of the largest independent alternative managers in Japan; parent listed on TOKYO PRO Market (7176)
new_firms.note: Independent Tokyo alternative/multi-strategy manager (founded 1999), historically fixed-income/derivatives, now multi-asset incl. systematic. Graded Systematic on its quant multi-asset sleeve.

new_firms.compute_signal: Explicitly ML/deep-learning-first: proprietary deep-neural-net stock-selection engine (built with LG AI Research), Kirin API data pipeline, Alpha Factory, AXE AI execution. Compute appears cloud/partner-based; no owned GPU/DC disclosed.
new_firms.scale: SoftBank-backed ($146m Series B, 2022); AI-driven NYSE ETFs; ~51-200 employees
new_firms.note: Flagship Korean ML-native asset-tech firm (founded 2016, CEO Marcus Hyung-sik Kim). Name = 'Quant' + 'Craft'. The clearest ML-native quant HQ in the region.

new_firms.scale: Largest Korean hedge-fund house by AUM in the domestic private-fund space
new_firms.note: Seoul multi-strategy manager (est. 2008) running equity L/S, event-driven, macro/CTA and quant sleeves under a multi-manager platform. Graded Multi-mgr. Also runs active-AI ETFs.

new_firms.compute_signal: ML-based platform: robo-advisor engine using ML + asset-allocation algorithms with dynamic rebalancing; Series C ($33.4m, 2021) to develop the ML platform. Compute not disclosed / likely cloud.
new_firms.scale: Raised ~$49-50m total; among the largest Korean robo-advisors by AUM
new_firms.note: Seoul fintech/quant asset manager (founded 2015) running ML-driven robo-advisory and discretionary allocation. Graded Quant AM. Credible-press source used to confirm profile.
Ultra-secretive stat-arb quant fund descended from Princeton-Newport (Thorp lineage). Founded 1989; offices Irvine CA and Princeton NJ. Runs internal capital only; discloses essentially nothing.
Multi-manager platform with deep systematic/quant roots (quant is the largest strategy in its Strategic Partners Fund). Founded 1988 by Steven Schonfeld. 1,000+ staff globally.
Multi-strategy platform that began in 2005 as a Minnesota options MM; restructured to take outside capital in 2017. HQ New York with London/Dubai. Quantitative is one of four core strategy groups.
Multi-strategy platform (Greenwich CT) founded 2008 by Nicholas Maounis (ex-Amaranth). Runs a Quantitative Strategies sleeve alongside credit/vol-arb/event-driven.

new_firms.compute_signal: none public (ML-forward stated; no DC/GPU disclosure)
new_firms.note: Founded 2003 by Jaffray Woodriff et al. in Charlottesville VA. Explicitly ML-driven short/medium-term forecasting. ~39 staff. ML-native by stated method; no public data-centre footprint.

new_firms.compute_signal: AI/ML research signal (autonomous trading agents, continuous-learning models per company site); no public DC/GPU disclosure
new_firms.note: Quant market maker / liquidity provider founded 2013, New York. Frames itself around AI researchers and 'autonomous trading agents'. Latency-first MM core; AI framing is a research signal but no disclosed compute campus.
Chicago-area (Northbrook IL) systematic investment manager founded 2010 by Sam Barnett (applied math/neuroscience PhD). Runs Polysight equity hedge funds.
Chicago proprietary automated market maker founded 2002. Quantitative automated MM across global futures, equities, commodities, options, fixed income, crypto. Latency/colo-first.
Chicago options/derivatives market maker founded 2002 (originated in the CBOE SPX pit). Makes markets in equity-index and commodity derivatives. Execution/colo-first.
Proprietary options market maker founded 1997 in Chicago by Matt Hulsizer and Jenny Just (ex-O'Connor). Parent PEAK6 reportedly relocated HQ to Austin TX in Dec 2024. Execution-first.
Chicago algorithmic/proprietary trading firm founded 2004 by Chris Lorenzen. Liquidity provider across listed derivatives and securities globally. Latency-first.
One of the largest privately held US options MMs; founded 1989 (San Francisco origin), now HQ Chicago. Options market maker/specialist across CBOE/NYSE/Nasdaq/MIAX/BOX. Execution-first.

new_firms.compute_signal: none public (alt-data / quant research stated; no DC/GPU disclosure)
new_firms.note: Chicago quant firm founded 2009 by Misha Malyshev (ex-Citadel global head of HFT). Evolved from pure HFT to a broader quant hedge fund (futures, equity stat-arb, options, alt data).
Chicago proprietary trading firm founded 2004, trades own capital only across 20+ global exchanges. Execution/latency-first. (2019 CFTC spoofing settlement on record.)
Prague-based algorithmic market maker (part of RSJ Group), founded 1994, algo trading since 2002. Futures MM in 50+ instruments on major US/EU derivatives exchanges. Execution/latency-first.
Amsterdam proprietary trading firm founded 2015; volatility/options MM plus position-taking. Also active as designated crypto MM (Kraken, MEXC). Execution/latency-first.
London proprietary trading firm founded 2011; options market-making plus systematic and discretionary strategies across global derivatives/equities. Execution/MM core.

Global multi-asset electronic trading firm incorporated 2010, New York. Technology + quantitative research + trading; backed by Technology Crossover Ventures. Lower-profile; verify current activity before mapping.

new_firms.compute_signal: on-prem AI supercomputer 'WALLACE' publicly announced (2023): fluorinert liquid-cooled proprietary system, datacentre-level cooling; multi-model evolutionary-computing platform
new_firms.note: Toronto (+NY) AI-first hedge fund. Explicitly ML-native: proprietary 'WALLACE' evolutionary/geno-synthetic system. Rare Canadian ML-native standalone with a disclosed hardware footprint (2023 GlobeNewswire announcement).

new_firms.compute_signal: none public (high-performance R&D platform stated; no DC/GPU disclosure)
new_firms.note: Chicago systematic hedge fund founded 2018/2019 by Jonathan Graham (ex-head of Citadel stat-arb / equity HFT). Emphasized high-performance research platform. Offices Chicago and NY.
New York quantitative investment manager founded 2014 (Glenn Dubin, chairman). Systematic multi-strategy on a proprietary platform.

Boston quantitative equity asset manager founded 1999. Systematic global/international equity (long-only, long/short, alpha-extension) for institutions; $200bn+ AUM. Very large quant AM often omitted from HFT-centric lists.
Swiss systematic managed-futures / trend-following CTA founded 2003 (Schaffhausen; team now in Zurich). 60+ liquid futures/FX instruments; CFTC-registered, FINMA-regulated.
Connecticut (Rowayton/Norwalk) alternative manager founded 1994 by Kenneth Tropin. Runs systematic quant models (trend, quant macro, diversifying) alongside discretionary global macro.

new_firms.compute_signal: none public (statistical-learning framework stated; no DC/GPU disclosure)
new_firms.note: One of the oldest systematic managers (roots 1971), New York. Phased out traditional trend-following in favor of a 'contextual statistical-learning' (ML) framework — an ML-native systematic case study.

new_firms.compute_signal: Cloud/ML compute — public Google Cloud customer case study describes using cloud CPU/GPU scale-out to develop quant signals and expand ML capability
new_firms.scale: ~70+ staff; DMM on SGX, JPX, CME; >US$500bn notional/month (self-reported)
new_firms.note: Singapore-HQ prop trading firm / market maker founded 2006 by John Lin. Ultra-low-latency execution plus systematic strategies. Cloud case study is a rare public research-compute signal for an APAC prop shop.

new_firms.scale: ~US$6bn AUM (reported); ~100 staff across Singapore and New York
new_firms.note: Singapore-born pure-systematic global-macro hedge fund founded 2006. Statistical models across bonds, equities, commodities, FX and insurance-linked securities. One of Singapore's largest home-grown funds.

Singapore-HQ proprietary trading firm founded 2010. Systematic, multi-asset (equities, commodities, regulated digital assets); market making, arbitrage, event-driven. Emphasises in-house infrastructure but discloses no compute/DC specifics.
Singapore-based quantitative proprietary trading firm; systematic research, automation and quantitative engineering with private capital. Small/private; no public compute or data-centre signal.

new_firms.scale: 120+ staff; offices HK, Sydney, Shanghai, Chicago
new_firms.note: Hong Kong-HQ proprietary equity-derivatives firm founded 2007. Options market maker across HK, Japan, Korea, Taiwan, Australia; delta-one, ETFs, commodity derivatives, some crypto. Latency/execution-focused.

new_firms.scale: 300+ staff; offices HK, Singapore, Zug; connected to 60+ venues
new_firms.note: Hong Kong-incorporated digital-asset market maker / liquidity provider founded 2013. Received Singapore crypto license 2024. Execution/liquidity engine; no public ML-compute or DC disclosure.

new_firms.scale: 150+ team members across ~15 geographies; connected to 40+ venues
new_firms.note: Hong Kong-HQ crypto algorithmic trading and market-making firm, spun out standalone Jan 2019. Latency/algorithmic execution; no public research-compute footprint.

new_firms.scale: Reportedly exceeded US$3bn AUM at peak
new_firms.note: Hong Kong-HQ quantitative FX hedge fund founded 2003 by Joe Zhou (PhD Finance, Wharton). Quantitative models across ~28 global currencies. Systematic macro/FX-focused, not a latency shop.

new_firms.scale: Teams in Australia, Hong Kong, Amsterdam; charitable-trust ownership
new_firms.note: Sydney-HQ proprietary trading firm founded 2011 by Rob Keldoulis. Market making, quantitative and positional trading across stocks, futures, options, ETFs, FX, crypto; APAC focus. Execution-first.

new_firms.scale: ~A$109m total revenue FY2024 (reported); offices Sydney, London, New York, Dubai
new_firms.note: Sydney-HQ proprietary trading firm founded 2007; converts discretionary decisions into systematic trades based on quantitative research and models. Firm capital (prop).

Australian proprietary trading firm founded 2006, HQ Austinmer/Wollongong NSW. Quantitative research across derivatives, FX, commodities, securities; co-designed a University of Wollongong quant-trading degree. Latency/execution-oriented.
Sydney-HQ electronic principal trading firm founded 2016; market making and arbitrage globally on a unified low-latency platform. Prop capital.

new_firms.scale: ~R$7bn AUM (reported); largest quant manager in Latin America; XP Inc. minority stake
new_firms.note: Sao Paulo-HQ, Brazil's largest systematic/quant asset manager, founded 2012 (originally Visia Investimentos, renamed 2019). Fully algorithmic multimarket funds (Zarathustra, Darius, Sigma, Axis).

new_firms.scale: ~24 staff; oldest quant manager in Brazil
new_firms.note: Rio de Janeiro-HQ quantitative asset manager founded 2007 — described as the oldest quant hedge-fund manager in Brazil. Systematic strategies (trend, behavioural-bias).

Rio de Janeiro-HQ quantitative multimarket manager founded 2008, one of Brazil's oldest quant houses. Trend-following and mean-reversion across Brazilian and North American liquid assets; states it uses AI to confirm/deny trades.

Buenos Aires-HQ quantitative manager (founder Francisco Prack) using algorithmic/model-driven strategies; flagship Argie BondQuant is a signal-driven ARS sovereign-bond portfolio. One of the few standalone quant shops in Spanish-speaking LatAm.

dropped.reason: Global-derivatives trading-services operation (part of Hertshten Group), not a self-alpha systematic fund; the enumerator itself flagged it as borderline/quant-adjacent with execution-only signal — folded out to keep the universe to genuine trading firms.

Primarily a low-latency trading-technology / solutions vendor (workstations, robo/charting platforms, 'accelerated box') serving other firms — a tech vendor, not a standalone trading firm; per task rule 3.
Explicitly a trading-technology vendor (multi-asset platforms, OMS/RMS, NSE/BSE-approved algo vendor), NOT a prop fund; per task rule 3 (drop pure tech vendors).

result.summary: Editted 82 enumerated records down to 79 NEW firms to add and 3 dropped. No cross-region or existing-table duplicates were found (the two 'Mingshi' entries — 蒙玺 Mengxi MM/HFT vs 鸣石 Mingshi Fund Systematic — are genuinely distinct firms and both kept). Dropped: 2 pure tech vendors (QuantX Technology, uTrade Solutions) and 1 trading-services/derivatives-broking operation flagged as non-alpha (Futures First). No defunct/acquired firms were present. firm_type left on the controlled vocabulary throughout; ADIA QRD kept as Systematic (an internal SWF desk, flagged in note); Estee kept as Quant AM per its distinguishing AM arm. Continent hygiene confirmed: all Israel/UAE/Saudi records correctly sit on 'Middle East', not 'Asia'. Counts by continent of the 79 kept firms — Asia: 40 (China 15, India 11, Japan 3, South Korea 3, Singapore 4, Hong Kong 4); Middle East: 8 (Israel 5, UAE 2, Saudi Arabia 1); North America: 20 (United States 18, Canada 1... plus US-based Eladian; note Continuum/ADIA are Middle East); Europe: 4 (Czech Republic 1, Netherlands 1, United Kingdom 1, Switzerland 1); Oceania: 4 (Australia); South America: 4 (Brazil 3, Argentina 1). Every surviving firm retains its source_url, a compute_signal, and a concise note.
